seoTitle: Chifley vs Chisholm — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Chifley and Chisholm. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Chifley and Chisholm.
heading: Chifley vs Chisholm — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chifley, Australian Capital Territory and Chisholm, Australian Capital Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Chisholm has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Chifley (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chifley or Chisholm safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chifley has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Chisholm scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chifley scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Chisholm scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chifley or Chisholm?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Chisholm has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
